content: Retrieval-augmented generation, often shortened to RAG, combines information retrieval with text generation so a model can answer using indexed external context.
A typical pipeline chunks source documents, builds embeddings, retrieves relevant chunks for a query, and provides those chunks to the model during response synthesis.
Vector databases store numeric embeddings and support nearest-neighbor search, enabling low-latency semantic retrieval for local assistants and edge deployments.
Distributed systems often separate orchestration from execution. A control plane coordinates tasks while worker processes execute isolated units of work.
Speech pipelines usually transcribe audio into text first, then apply language understanding and generation models for downstream actions such as summaries or commands.
